---
title: "Framework Documentation"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

RECAST is built as a set of Claude Code slash commands and internal skills. All orchestration is done by AI agents communicating through the filesystem — no custom Python runtime is required to operate the framework.

## Methodological foundations

The RECAST pipeline is grounded in two key academic references:

- **DML methodology:** [Baiardi & Naghi (2024)](https://doi.org/10.1093/ectj/utae004), "The value added of machine learning to causal inference: evidence from revisited studies," *Econometrics Journal*. The pipeline's DML extension — 7 ML methods, adaptive K-fold cross-fitting, median aggregation across sample splits, B&N adjusted standard errors, BLP/GATE/CLAN heterogeneity analysis — is directly based on their approach and replication code.

- **Referee process:** [Berk, Harvey & Hirshleifer (2017)](https://doi.org/10.1257/jep.31.1.231), "How to Write an Effective Referee Report and Improve the Scientific Review Process," *Journal of Economic Perspectives*. The review loop follows their principles: contribution assessment before criticism, essential vs. suggested separation with scientific justification required, cost-benefit weighting, the implicit revise-and-resubmit bargain, and the synthesis referee as quality control against signal-jamming.

::: {.docs-grid}

::: {.doc-card}
### [Pipeline Architecture](framework/pipeline-diagram.qmd)
Visual diagram of the full pipeline — from paper ingestion to published report, including both DML and Causal Forest paths.
:::

::: {.doc-card}
### [Quick Start](framework/quickstart.qmd)
Install, scaffold a project, add your files, and launch the pipeline in four steps.
:::

::: {.doc-card}
### [Pipeline Stages](framework/pipeline.qmd)
Reference for each of the six analysis notebooks, the Advisor Gate, the Review Loop, and the Final Referee.
:::

::: {.doc-card}
### [Slash Commands](framework/commands.qmd)
Usage and rules for `/recast`, `/recast-cf`, `/init`, `/stage`, `/review`, `/final`, and `/publish`.
:::

::: {.doc-card}
### [Skills Reference](framework/skills.qmd)
Internal sub-agent skills: orchestrator, notebook runner, gate validators, referees, synthesis, and revision agent.
:::

::: {.doc-card}
### [Project Structure](framework/paper-structure.qmd)
The folder layout created by `/init`, immutability rules, and what to read when the pipeline finishes.
:::

:::
